keywords.description: function - Declares a function. Functions are reusable blocks of code that can be called with arguments.
var - Declares a variable with function scope. Hoisted and can be redeclared. Use let/const instead for modern code.
let - Declares a block-scoped variable that can be reassigned. Preferred over var for mutable variables.
const - Declares a block-scoped constant that cannot be reassigned. Use for values that won't change.
if - Conditional statement that executes code based on a boolean condition. Foundation of program logic.
else - Provides alternative execution path when if condition evaluates to false.
for - Loop that repeats code a specific number of times with initialization, condition, and increment.
while - Loop that repeats while a condition remains true. Use when iteration count is unknown.
return - Returns a value from a function and immediately exits function execution.
class - Declares a class for object-oriented programming. Modern way to create constructor functions.
extends - Creates a class that inherits from another class. Enables classical inheritance patterns.
super - Refers to the parent class. Used to call parent constructor or methods in inheritance.
this - Refers to the current object instance. Context-dependent and changes based on how function is called.
new - Creates a new instance of a class or constructor function. Invokes constructor and returns object.
try - Begins a block that might throw an exception. Must be followed by catch and/or finally.
catch - Handles exceptions thrown in a try block. Allows graceful error recovery.
finally - Code that always executes after try/catch, regardless of success or failure.
throw - Throws an exception/error that can be caught by try/catch blocks.
async - Declares an asynchronous function that returns a Promise. Enables use of await keyword.
await - Waits for a Promise to resolve. Can only be used inside async functions.
import - Imports modules, functions, or variables from other files. Part of ES6 module system.
export - Exports functions, variables, or classes to be used in other modules.
default - Used with export/import for default module exports and switch statement default cases.
from - Specifies the module path when importing. Used with import statements.
as - Renames imports/exports or used for type assertions in TypeScript.
instanceof - Tests if an object is an instance of a specific constructor/class.
typeof - Returns a string indicating the type of the operand.
in - Tests if a property exists in an object or iterates over object properties.
delete - Removes a property from an object. Use with caution as it affects performance.
void - Evaluates an expression and returns undefined. Often used as void(0).
null - Represents the intentional absence of any object value. Primitive value.
undefined - Represents an uninitialized or missing value. Default for unassigned variables.
true - Boolean literal representing logical truth. Falsy values convert to false.
false - Boolean literal representing logical falsehood.
break - Exits from a loop or switch statement immediately.
keywords.example: for (let i = 0; i < 10; i++) { if (i === 5) break; }

keywords.description: continue - Skips the rest of the current loop iteration and continues with the next.
keywords.example: for (let i = 0; i < 10; i++) { if (i % 2 === 0) continue; }

keywords.description: do - Part of do-while loop. Executes code at least once before checking condition.
switch - Multi-branch conditional statement. More efficient than multiple if-else for many conditions.
case - Defines a branch in a switch statement. Must be followed by break to prevent fall-through.
with - Extends scope chain with an object. Deprecated and not allowed in strict mode.
debugger - Invokes debugging functionality. Causes breakpoint in developer tools.
constructor - Special method for creating and initializing class instances.
static - Defines static methods/properties that belong to the class, not instances.
get - Defines a getter method that appears as a property but executes code when accessed.
set - Defines a setter method that executes when a property is assigned a value.
yield - Pauses generator function execution and returns a value to the caller.
of - Used in for-of loops to iterate over iterable objects (arrays, strings, etc.).
Symbol - Creates unique primitive values. Often used for object property keys.
BigInt - Represents integers larger than Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER.
Array - Global object for creating arrays. Provides array methods and properties.
Object - Base constructor for all objects. Provides utility methods like Object.keys().
String - Global object for working with strings. Provides string methods and properties.
Number - Global object for working with numbers. Provides number methods and constants.
Boolean - Global object for working with boolean values. Can convert values to boolean.
Date - Creates date objects for working with dates and times.
RegExp - Creates regular expression objects for pattern matching in strings.
Error - Creates error objects. Base class for all JavaScript errors.
TypeError - Error thrown when a value is not of the expected type.
ReferenceError - Error thrown when referencing an undefined variable.
SyntaxError - Error thrown when JavaScript engine encounters invalid syntax.
Promise - Represents eventual completion or failure of asynchronous operation.
Map - Collection of keyed values where keys can be any type. Maintains insertion order.
Set - Collection of unique values of any type. Automatically removes duplicates.
WeakMap - Collection of key-value pairs where keys are objects and weakly referenced.
WeakSet - Collection of unique objects that are weakly referenced.
Proxy - Intercepts and customizes operations performed on objects (property lookup, assignment, etc.).
Reflect - Provides methods for interceptable JavaScript operations. Used with Proxy.
JSON - Global object for parsing and stringifying JSON data.
Math - Global object providing mathematical constants and functions.
console - Global object for logging and debugging. Available in browsers and Node.js.
window - Global object in browsers representing the browser window. Contains DOM APIs.
document - Represents the HTML document in browsers. Entry point for DOM manipulation.
global - Global object in Node.js. Similar to window in browsers.
process - Node.js global providing information about and control over the current process.
Buffer - Node.js global for handling binary data. Similar to Uint8Array.
require - Node.js function for importing modules. CommonJS module system.
module - Node.js object representing the current module. Has exports property.
exports - Node.js shorthand for module.exports. Used to export values from modules.
__dirname - Node.js global containing directory name of current module.
__filename - Node.js global containing filename of current module.
setTimeout - Executes a function after a specified delay in milliseconds.
setInterval - Executes a function repeatedly with a fixed delay between calls.
clearTimeout - Cancels a timeout previously established by setTimeout.
clearInterval - Cancels an interval previously established by setInterval.
fetch - Modern API for making HTTP requests. Returns a Promise that resolves to Response.
localStorage - Browser API for storing data locally with no expiration time.
sessionStorage - Browser API for storing data for the duration of the page session.
addEventListener - DOM method to register event listeners on elements.
removeEventListener - DOM method to remove previously registered event listeners.
globalThis - Universal global object that works in any environment (browser, Node.js, workers). Preferred over window/global.
structuredClone - Creates a deep clone of a value using the structured clone algorithm. Works with most built-in types.
queueMicrotask - Queues a microtask to run after current task completes but before rendering. Higher priority than setTimeout.
requestAnimationFrame - Schedules a callback before the next browser repaint. Ideal for animations at 60fps.
cancelAnimationFrame - Cancels an animation frame request previously scheduled with requestAnimationFrame.
requestIdleCallback - Schedules a callback to run during browser idle periods. Good for non-urgent background work.
AbortController - Creates a controller object to abort one or more web requests (fetch, event listeners, streams).
keywords.example: const controller = new AbortController(); fetch(url, { signal: controller.signal });

keywords.description: AbortSignal - Signal object used to communicate with and abort asynchronous operations. Created via AbortController.
TextEncoder - Encodes strings into Uint8Array using UTF-8 encoding.
keywords.example: const encoder = new TextEncoder(); const bytes = encoder.encode('hello');

keywords.description: TextDecoder - Decodes Uint8Array or ArrayBuffer into strings. Supports multiple encodings.
keywords.example: const decoder = new TextDecoder('utf-8'); const text = decoder.decode(bytes);

keywords.description: URL - Parses and constructs URLs with access to individual components (host, pathname, search params).
URLSearchParams - Utility for working with URL query strings. Supports iteration and manipulation.
keywords.example: const params = new URLSearchParams({ q: 'search', page: '1' });

keywords.description: FormData - Constructs key/value pairs representing form fields. Used with fetch for multipart/form-data uploads.
keywords.example: const form = new FormData(); form.append('file', blob, 'filename.txt');

keywords.description: Headers - Represents HTTP request/response headers. Used with fetch API.
Response - Represents the response to a fetch request. Provides methods to read body as JSON, text, blob, etc.
Request - Represents a resource request. Used with fetch API and service workers.
keywords.example: const req = new Request('/api/data', { method: 'POST', body: JSON.stringify(data) });

keywords.description: IntersectionObserver - Observes when elements enter or leave the viewport. Used for lazy loading, infinite scroll.
keywords.example: const observer = new IntersectionObserver((entries) => { entries.forEach(e => e.isIntersecting && load(e.target)); });

keywords.description: MutationObserver - Watches for changes to the DOM tree (attributes, child nodes, text content).
keywords.example: const observer = new MutationObserver((mutations) => { mutations.forEach(m => handle(m)); });

keywords.description: ResizeObserver - Observes changes to element dimensions. More efficient than polling or resize events.
keywords.example: const observer = new ResizeObserver((entries) => { entries.forEach(e => updateLayout(e.contentRect)); });

keywords.description: PerformanceObserver - Observes performance measurement events (paint, navigation, resource loading).
keywords.example: const observer = new PerformanceObserver((list) => { list.getEntries().forEach(e => log(e)); });

keywords.description: performance - High-resolution timing API for measuring code performance and page load metrics.
keywords.example: const start = performance.now(); doWork(); console.log(performance.now() - start);

keywords.description: navigator - Browser API providing information about the user agent, geolocation, permissions, and more.
Intl - Internationalization API for locale-sensitive formatting of numbers, dates, and strings.
WeakRef - Holds a weak reference to an object that doesn't prevent garbage collection. ES2021.
keywords.example: const weakRef = new WeakRef(largeObject); const obj = weakRef.deref();

keywords.description: FinalizationRegistry - Registers callbacks to run when objects are garbage collected. ES2021.
ArrayBuffer - Fixed-length raw binary data buffer. Foundation for typed arrays and DataView.
SharedArrayBuffer - Binary data buffer that can be shared between workers for parallel computation.
DataView - Low-level interface for reading/writing multiple number types in an ArrayBuffer.
Uint8Array - Typed array of 8-bit unsigned integers. Common for binary data, file I/O, and crypto.
Atomics - Provides atomic operations on SharedArrayBuffer for thread-safe parallel programming.
AggregateError - Error wrapping multiple errors into one. Thrown by Promise.any() when all promises reject.
keywords.example: throw new AggregateError([error1, error2], 'All promises rejected');

keywords.description: RangeError - Error thrown when a value is not within the expected range.
keywords.example: throw new RangeError('Index out of bounds');

keywords.description: URIError - Error thrown when a URI handling function is used incorrectly.
EvalError - Error relating to the global eval() function. Rarely used in modern code.
Iterator - Protocol for creating custom iterable objects. Requires a next() method returning { value, done }.
keywords.example: const iter = { [Symbol.iterator]() { let i = 0; return { next() { return { value: i++, done: i > 3 }; } }; } };

keywords.description: ReadableStream - Represents a readable stream of data. Used by fetch response body and other streaming APIs.
keywords.example: const stream = new ReadableStream({ start(controller) { controller.enqueue('chunk'); controller.close(); } });

keywords.description: WritableStream - Represents a writable destination for streaming data.
keywords.example: const stream = new WritableStream({ write(chunk) { process(chunk); } });

keywords.description: TransformStream - A pair of readable and writable streams that transforms data flowing through.
keywords.example: const { readable, writable } = new TransformStream({ transform(chunk, controller) { controller.enqueue(chunk.toUpperCase()); } });

keywords.description: BroadcastChannel - Enables messaging between browsing contexts (tabs, iframes, workers) on the same origin.
keywords.example: const channel = new BroadcastChannel('app'); channel.postMessage({ type: 'update' });

keywords.description: MessageChannel - Creates a two-port message channel for communicating between different contexts.
keywords.example: const { port1, port2 } = new MessageChannel(); port1.onmessage = (e) => handle(e.data);

keywords.description: Blob - Represents immutable raw binary data. Used for file creation, downloads, and data URLs.
File - Extends Blob with file metadata (name, lastModified). Represents files from input or drag-and-drop.
FileReader - Asynchronously reads File or Blob contents as text, data URL, or ArrayBuffer.
keywords.example: const reader = new FileReader(); reader.onload = (e) => console.log(e.target.result); reader.readAsText(file);

keywords.description: crypto - Web Crypto API for cryptographic operations. Provides randomUUID() and SubtleCrypto methods.
